title: Time Picker
subtitle: Component for selecting time with multiple modes, sizes and advanced configurations

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple time picker with default configuration. Select hours and minutes in 24-hour format.

modes.title: Time modes
modes.description: Five available modes: hours only (12h/24h), hours and minutes (12h/24h), and duration mode.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five size options that harmoniously scale the component and its internal elements.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the height, padding and font size of the picker. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg' and 'xl'.

presets.title: Quick presets
presets.description: Add preset buttons for frequent times like 'Now', 'Breakfast', 'Lunch'. Can include custom icons.
presets.note: Provide an array of TimePreset objects in the config. Each preset has a label, time and optional icon. Supports Remix Icon icons.

steps.title: Hour and minute steps
steps.description: Control the increment intervals for hours and minutes to limit selectable options.
steps.note: Use hourStep and minuteStep in the config. For example, hourStep: 2 shows only even hours, and minuteStep: 15 shows quarter hours (00, 15, 30, 45).

timeRange.title: Time range
timeRange.description: Restricts the selectable time range using minimum and maximum values. Optionally shows a visual indicator.
timeRange.note: Configure minTime and maxTime in the config. Times outside this range are disabled. Use showRangeIndicator: true to visually highlight the valid range.

disabledTimes.title: Disabled time
disabledTimes.description: Blocks specific hours or minutes, preventing their selection. Useful for unavailable time slots.
disabledTimes.note: Use disabledHours and disabledMinutes in the config to specify arrays of disabled values. Disabled items show a distinctive dimmed style.

durationMode.title: Duration mode
durationMode.description: Special mode for selecting time intervals. Can display seconds and set a maximum duration.
durationMode.note: Set mode='DURATION' and configure with duration: { maxHours: 8, showSeconds: true } in the config. Useful for shift selection, time tracking, etc.

defaultStrategies.title: Default strategies
defaultStrategies.description: Controls the initial time when no value is provided: now, smart (current + offset), empty or custom.
defaultStrategies.note: Use defaultStrategy with values 'now', 'smart', 'empty' or 'custom'. For 'smart', configure smartOffset in minutes. For 'custom', use defaultValue.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Disables the entire picker, preventing user interaction and showing a dimmed appearance.
disabled.note: Use the disabled property to dynamically control whether the time picker is enabled or disabled.

reactiveForms.title: Reactive forms
reactiveForms.description: Full integration with Angular reactive forms. Implements ControlValueAccessor.
reactiveForms.note: Use formControl or formControlName to integrate with Angular forms. The component handles synchronization, validation and state automatically.

overlayIntegration.title: Overlay integration
overlayIntegration.description: Use autoClose and selectFinished to integrate the TimeSelector inside a datepicker or popover. The selectFinished output is emitted when selection is complete so the host can close the overlay.
overlayIntegration.note: The overlayMode input activates additional focus behaviors designed for when the component is rendered inside an overlay: automatically focuses the first column on mount.

customFooter.title: Custom footer
customFooter.description: Project an ng-template with the nuiTimeSelectorFooter directive to replace the built-in footer with your own action buttons, additional info or any content you need.
customFooter.note: The custom template replaces the built-in footer ('Clear' and 'Now' buttons). The typed context TimeSelectorFooterContext exposes $implicit (current value), actions with clear() and setToNow() methods, normalization and range.

customHeader.title: Custom header
customHeader.description: Project an ng-template with the nuiTimeSelectorHeader directive to replace the visual display of the header with your own content: formatted value, range indicator, normalization alert or any element you need.
customHeader.note: The custom template only replaces the visual content of the header. Accessibility live regions (aria-live) remain active regardless, ensuring screen readers continue announcing value changes. The typed context TimeSelectorHeaderContext exposes $implicit (current value), formattedTime (readable text), mode, is12h, normalization and range.

customItem.title: Custom item
customItem.description: Project an ng-template with the nuiTimeSelectorItem directive to visually customize the content of each option in the hour, minute and second columns.
customItem.note: The directive only replaces the visual content of the item button. The component still manages all ARIA attributes (role='option', aria-selected), keyboard navigation and selection events. The context includes the value as $implicit, the column type type, and the selected and disabled states.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior, appearance and restrictions of the time picker

rows.autoClose: If true, emits selectFinished when the selection is complete. Designed for use inside a datepicker or overlay.
rows.overlayMode: If true, activates overlay-specific behaviors: auto-focuses the first column when opened.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the time picker when the user interacts with it

rows.selectFinished: Emits (void) when the user completes the selection and autoClose is true. The overlay can close upon receiving this event.

config.title: TimeSelectorConfig
config.description: Configuration interface with all available options: steps, ranges, disabled times, presets and duration configuration

durationConfig.title: DurationConfig
durationConfig.description: Specific configuration for DURATION mode: hour limits, show seconds, steps and quick duration presets

models.title: Type definitions
models.description: Interfaces and type definitions for values, configuration and presets

methods.title: Methods
methods.description: The component implements ControlValueAccessor for seamless integration with Angular forms

contentDirectives.title: Content directives
contentDirectives.description: Marker directives that allow projecting custom templates for items, the footer and the header of the time picker

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: CSS color variables generated by ThemeService for each semantic color. Each color generates 9 specific variables for different picker states: item backgrounds (normal, hover, selected), text colors, disabled states, accents and focus ring.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Size-specific CSS variables that control item heights, paddings and container dimensions. Five size variants available: xs, sm, md (default), lg and xl.

structure.title: Structure
structure.description: Structural CSS variables to control dimensions, spacing, typography and visual appearance. Includes separator style, scrollbar customization, borders, fonts and focus states.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Examples of how to customize time picker styles in your application. You can override CSS variables globally or specific to instances. The integration with ThemeService allows generating color variables for your custom semantic colors.

tokens.title: Time picker tokens
tokens.description: Tokens used for time picker labels, action buttons and screen reader texts.
tokens.note: Override any token globally with provideNuiI18n({ timeSelector: { ... } }).

setToCurrentTime.description: Label for the 'set to current time' action button.

selectTime.description: Placeholder label when no time is selected.

clear.description: Label for the clear/reset time action.

hour.description: Header label for the hour column (long form).

minutes.description: Header label for the minutes column (long form).

hour12.description: Label used when the picker is in 12-hour mode.

hour24.description: Label used when the picker is in 24-hour mode.

period.description: Label for the AM/PM period column.

normalizedTime.description: Screen reader announcement template for the normalized time value.

rangeLabel.description: Accessible label describing a time range selection.

timeSelected.description: Screen reader announcement when a time has been successfully selected.

durationAndPresets.title: Duration and preset tokens
durationAndPresets.description: Specific tokens for duration mode labels, column headers and preset names.
durationAndPresets.note: Duration tokens live under the timeSelector.duration namespace and preset tokens under timeSelector.presets.

hours.description: Long form of 'hours' in the duration display.

minutes.description: Long form of 'minutes' in the duration display.

seconds.description: Long form of 'seconds' in the duration display.

selectDuration.description: Placeholder label in duration selection mode.

durationSelected.description: Screen reader announcement when a duration has been selected.

durationHours.description: Screen reader announcement for the hours value in duration mode.

durationMinutes.description: Screen reader announcement for the minutes value in duration mode.

durationSeconds.description: Screen reader announcement for the seconds value in duration mode.

tabSelector.description: Label for the manual selector tab when both selection and preset tabs are visible.

tabPresets.description: Label for the presets tab when both tabs are visible.

presetsMinutes.description: Label template for minute-based presets (e.g. '15 minutes').

presetsHours.description: Label template for multi-hour presets (e.g. '2 hours').

a11yTokens.title: Accessibility tokens
a11yTokens.description: Tokens used exclusively for screen reader announcements and ARIA labels within the time picker.

timeSelectorLabel.description: The aria-label applied to the time picker root container in standard mode.

durationPickerLabel.description: The aria-label applied to the root container in duration mode.

hourColumnHelp.description: Help text announced by the screen reader for the hour column.

minuteColumnHelp.description: Help text announced for the minutes column.

periodHelp.description: Help text announced for the AM/PM column.

normalizedAlert.description: Text announced via an assertive live region when the selected time is normalized to the nearest valid value.

rangeInfo.description: Screen reader text describing the time range limits.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The TimeSelector implements the ARIA listbox / option pattern for each scrollable column.

containerLabel.description: The picker root element has aria-label set from the i18n token timeSelectorLabel (or durationPickerLabel).

columnRole.description: Each column has role="listbox" and an aria-label derived from its specific i18n token (hour, minutes, period…).

optionRole.description: Each time value has role="option" and aria-selected="true" when it is the active value.

columnLabels.description: Column help text tokens are announced when the column receives focus, indicating how to interact (arrow keys, Home/End).

optionSelected.description: When a time is selected, it is announced via the i18n token timeSelected which includes the selected time (e.g. 'Time selected: 3:15 PM').

normalizedAlert.description: When the time is normalized, it is announced via the i18n token normalizedTime which includes the normalized time (e.g. 'Time normalized to 4:00 PM').

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The picker and each column are labelled via i18n tokens. Individual options are labelled by their visible time value.
naming.note: Always configure i18n tokens for your language so screen reader users hear meaningful column labels (e.g. 'Hour', 'Minutes', 'AM/PM').

container.description: The root container label comes from timeSelector.a11y.timeSelectorLabel (or durationPickerLabel). Override it per language.

columns.description: Each column label comes from the corresponding i18n token, giving context to screen reader users for each scrollable region.

help.description: Column help text tokens are announced when the column receives focus, indicating how to interact (arrow keys, Home/End).

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: Each TimeSelector column behaves as an independent listbox. Use the following keys to navigate and select values.

arrows.description: Moves focus to the previous/next option within the focused column.

tab.description: Moves focus to the next interactive column or control (e.g. period toggler, action buttons).

homeEnd.description: Jumps to the first/last option in the focused column.

enterSpace.description: Selects the focused option and updates the displayed time value.

escape.description: Closes the time picker panel and reverts to the previously confirmed value.

templates.title: Custom templates and accessibility
templates.description: When using nuiTimeSelectorItem, nuiTimeSelectorFooter or nuiTimeSelectorHeader, the component maintains its accessibility infrastructure. Follow these guidelines to ensure an accessible experience.
templates.note: Never remove visible text from items when customizing. Screen readers announce button values via their text content. If you use only icons or colors, always add a span with class='sr-only' containing the readable value.

buttonWrapper.description: The outer button element (with role='option', aria-selected and keyboard management) is not modified. Only the inner content is replaced.

visibleText.description: Always include the readable value ({{ value }}) in the template. If the design hides it visually, use <span class='sr-only'>{{ value }}</span>.

footerButtons.description: Buttons in the custom footer must include type='button' and descriptive text or aria-label. Avoid icon-only buttons without a label.

headerLiveRegions.description: When using nuiTimeSelectorHeader, the live regions (aria-live='polite' for the current value and aria-live='assertive' for the normalization alert) remain in the DOM even when the template replaces the visual display. There is no need to add them manually in the custom template.

colorOnly.info: Don't use color alone to convey state
colorOnly.description: If the selected state is indicated only by color, users with low vision or color blindness won't be able to distinguish it. Always combine color with a typographic change or icon.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: The TimeSelector component supports global configuration via provideNUI(). Define default values once at the application level for all time pickers.
setup.note: Global configuration acts as default values and can always be overridden per instance via the component's inputs.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: The following properties can be configured globally via TimeSelectorGlobalConfig:

rows.showHeader: Whether to display the header showing the formatted time at the top of the picker.
rows.showFooter: Whether to display the built-in footer with the 'Clear' and 'Now' buttons.
rows.defaultStrategy: Initial value strategy: empty (no value), now (current time), smart (current time + offset) or custom.
rows.smartOffset: Offset in minutes added to the current time when strategy is smart.
rows.hourStep: Increment for the hour selector. For example, 2 shows only even hours.
rows.minuteStep: Increment for the minute selector. For example, 15 shows quarter hours.

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: NUI resolves configuration values using the following priority chain (highest priority wins):
